title: muscari botryoides subsp. motelayi
intro: Muscari botryoides subsp. motelayi, also known as Motelay's grape hyacinth, is a flowering plant species within the Asparagaceae family. It is primarily recognized for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The plant is believed to possess health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, Motelay's grape hyacinth has been used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which may be used to aid in treating respiratory conditions like bronchitis and coughs.
